items.title: ADOS Profile
items.description: Durable ADOS profiling system for classifying, summarizing, and validating extracted chat-transcript items with controlled archetypes, domains, schemas, ledgers, and evidence packs.

primary_archetype.label: Controlled spec / schema / glossary
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered value is a governed ADOS profile specification with schemas, controlled vocabulary, archetype/domain rules, and validation/evidence structure, not primarily a runnable app.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: Includes profile skills, directives, and prompt behavior contracts for consistent LLM classification.
Defines structured transformation of chat transcript material into profile outputs, ledgers, and evidence artifacts.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on controlled LLM classification behavior, drift guards, prompt contracts, and semantic consistency across transcript-derived outputs.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: Schemas and profile artifacts support repeatable extraction, validation, and structured data processing.
Governance, traceability, and evidence-ledger requirements materially affect validation and control.

items.goal: Maintain a controlled ADOS profiling system that turns reduced chat transcripts into consistent, evidence-backed project classifications and summaries.

objectives.text: Define stable archetype, domain, objective, requirement, delivery, and evidence vocabulary for ADOS profile outputs.
Package schemas, prompts, skills, and release bundles so profile generation can be repeated across many conversations and versions.
Use ledgers, QA evidence, drift guards, and validation rules to preserve traceability and semantic consistency.

requirements: Use controlled ADOS archetype and domain identifiers.
Classify the delivered artifact by reusable kind, not incidental files or implementation technology.
Preserve governed schema fields and archetype-specific contracts.
Support evidence-backed QA, ledgers, and release packaging.
Handle multiple versions and revisions without semantic drift.

requirements_evolution.change: Project activity begins.
Project activity continues through many profile, schema, QA, skill, and release bundles.
Expanded from profile schemas into first-release builds, QA evidence, requirements packs, and profile skill packaging.

archetype_fields.scope: ADOS profile schemas, controlled vocabulary, classification rules, evidence ledgers, QA requirements, and profile skill behavior for transcript-derived project summaries.
archetype_fields.how_to_update: Revise schemas, controlled vocabulary, drift guards, requirements packs, and QA evidence together; validate changes against existing profile outputs before release.

items.title: ScholaSpark SAT Navigator repository snapshot
items.description: Durable work on a React/Vite SAT preparation app, including College Browser usability improvements, Smart Practice controls, Projected SAT score logic, Supabase/Lovable schema reconciliation, release hygiene, tests, documentation, and agent handoff prompts.

primary_archetype.label: User-facing application
primary_archetype.rationale: The durable delivery is a runnable SAT preparation web app whose acceptance turns on UI behavior, routing, practice workflows, scoring logic, tests, and database-backed features.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: Multiple Cursor and Lovable prompts define implementation, review, schema reconciliation, and handoff workflows.
Database schema reconciliation docs, security requirements, Cursor constraints, and Lovable action docs govern implementation and review.
Audit and generation scripts support repository hygiene, generated-file detection, and question-bank metadata workflows.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness is governed by SAT practice, readiness estimation, score projection, college targeting, and student motivation, not merely by the software stack.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: React/Vite app architecture, routing, tests, CI/build behavior, component refactors, and performance tuning materially affect delivery.
Question-bank metadata generation, static question loading, and generated artifact handling are material to app behavior and maintenance.
Supabase credentials, RLS, immutable responses, environment variables, and security docs are recurring correctness and risk concerns.
Controlled workflow rules govern what Cursor may change versus what Lovable must change, especially database schema and permissions.

items.goal: Maintain and prepare ScholaSpark SAT Navigator for release as a reliable, secure, maintainable SAT preparation app with accurate scoring, usable practice flows, and reviewable database changes.

objectives.text: Improve College Browser, Practice, Smart Practice, dashboard, projected SAT score, and badges for student usability and motivation.
Refactor code, reduce unnecessary tests, optimize generated assets, improve virtualization, URL state, and maintainability.
Keep database, security, Lovable handoff, Cursor constraints, generated-file rules, and review reports traceable and auditable.

requirements: Show filtered and total college counts in College Browser.
Allow selecting multiple SAT bands.
Delay ZIP filter application and ZIP validation until Save ZIP or max distance change.
Use reducer-based filter state, URL sync, filtering indexes, and virtualization where appropriate.
Tests must pass locally without Supabase environment configured.
Cursor must not perform git operations, Supabase schema changes, RLS changes, or Lovable dashboard changes unless explicitly allowed.
Database schema and permissions changes must be managed through Lovable Cloud and recorded in migrations and reports.
Keep diffs focused unless explicitly doing pre-release refactor work.
Ensure generated files are identifiable, ignored or tracked by clear rules, and removable.
Improve Projected SAT score logic so it reflects SAT readiness and motivates improvement.
Update user guide and docs to match implemented SAT calculation and practice-flow behavior.
Keep tracked environment configuration free of real secrets even if a temporary .env is retained.

requirements_evolution.change: Started with College Browser usability enhancements and review of Cursor implementations.
Added guidance for GitHub connector use and repo snapshot workarounds when connector access failed.
Shifted to verifying Cursor work, fixing Supabase-env test failures, and improving static question loading.
Added persistent Cursor constraints, Lovable handoff rules, and database/schema documentation expectations.
Expanded Lovable workflow from audit-only to schema reconciliation with before/after reports and migrations.
Handled rollback planning for main branch and preserving recent changes on an investigation branch.
Added pre-release refactor goals for compactness, test reduction, generated-file hygiene, .gitignore, and docs.
Reviewed release hygiene, temporary .env handling, dependencies, CI, and metadata typing.
Focused on Projected SAT score formulas, Smart Practice controls, Practice UX, badges, and user-guide accuracy.

archetype_fields.quickstart: Install dependencies, run tests/build, then run the Vite dev app; exact commands are supplied in project docs and prompts.
archetype_fields.how_to_use: Students use the web app for SAT practice, score readiness feedback, college browsing, filters, progress tracking, and related study workflows.
archetype_fields.how_to_update: Use Cursor for app code, tests, docs, and focused refactors; use Lovable Cloud for Supabase schema/RLS/env changes; record database changes in migrations and review reports.
key_features: Projected SAT score and section scoring

items.title: Holiday portrait transformation
items.description: Repeated image-editing requests to turn uploaded photos into warm, polished holiday portraits while preserving the subjects' real facial identity.

primary_archetype.label: Media / image generation
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered item is an edited/generated portrait image with specified visual style and constraints.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on portrait-editing aesthetics, lighting, composition, holiday styling, and faithful facial preservation.

items.goal: Create elegant, realistic holiday portrait transformations of uploaded photos while preserving the subjects' actual faces and expressions.

objectives.text: Preserve the real face, skin tone, expression, and features of the uploaded subject.
Apply warm holiday lighting, festive decor, and classic holiday-card colors and textures.
Iterate on portrait details such as eyes, shadows, sunglasses, Santa flavor, and magazine-style composition.

requirements: Use the uploaded photo as the source image.
Keep the person's real face, skin tone, expression, and features exactly the same.
Use soft holiday lighting with gentle golden highlights.
Add evergreen branches, warm twinkle lights, subtle bokeh glow, rich reds, forest greens, and cozy textures.
Make the final image elegant, warm, timeless, and not cartoonish.
Some variants requested Santa flavor, sunglasses, improved eyes, closer facial likeness, removal of shadow near the left eye, and a Time magazine pair-of-the-year style portrait.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial repeated request for a warm polished holiday portrait transformation preserving facial identity.
Added Santa flavor and sunglasses in one variant.
Requested improved eyes, closer match to original face, and removal of a shadow near the subject's left eye.
Requested a Time magazine pair of the 2025 year photo variant.
One generation attempt failed due to an image-generation tool error.

archetype_fields.subject: Uploaded person or subjects transformed into holiday portrait style.
archetype_fields.style: Warm polished realistic holiday portrait with golden highlights, evergreen branches, twinkle lights, bokeh, rich reds, forest greens, and cozy classic-card textures.
archetype_fields.constraints: Preserve real face, skin tone, expression, and features; avoid cartoonish output; accommodate requested variants such as Santa flavor, sunglasses, eye fixes, shadow removal, and magazine-style composition.

Includes validation and snapshot tooling such as docset checks, hash snapshots, tests, and pack validators.
Uses expert reviews, standards research, market/trend checks, and technology comparisons to shape the specification.

ISO/IEC/IEEE alignment, evidence requirements, auditability, and control manuals govern acceptance.

items.goal: Create a deterministic, human-reviewable ADOS/AiDos SDLC specification and documentation foundation that can later generate reliable Cursor agents, MCP tools, Skills, and Dark Factory project instances.

Convert documentation into controlled agent setup, Cursor MCP/Skills configuration, SDLC XML, pack schemas, and instance templates.

Provide runbooks, master documents, templates, and zip packages to speed continuation by the user or another AI agent.

requirements: Use ADOS/AiDos naming consistently and avoid uncontrolled ADIOS/ADOS/AIFirstSDLC drift.
Treat foundation documentation and standards as the single source of truth before finalizing XML, MCP server, and Cursor Skills.
Define agent roles, phase gates, stabilizers, success factors, and human approval points explicitly.
Align with 2026 ISO/IEC/IEEE standards, industry trends, Cursor MCP, Cursor Skills, and agentic software development practices.
Support WSL2-focused Cursor usage and setup documentation.
Include canonical project status, completion plan, reference guide, standards cookbook, foundation docs, and master merged Markdown.
Add Market Go/No-Go before BRD with BLUE/RED ocean, TAM/SAM/SOM, SourceIDs, $1M/year feasibility rule, and RequiredShare formula.
Ensure ADOS_PROJECT and DARKFACTORY folder placement is canonical.
Define Dark Factory instance layout, generation checklist, pack schemas, mappings, templates, validators, and tests.
Use deterministic validation, traceability, evidence links, and review gates before downstream generation.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial AIFirstSDLC/DOS enhancements targeted XML, Cursor MCP server setup, tool contracts, phase gating, memory, budgets, observability, and tests.
Expanded scope to Cursor MCP versus Skills setup, AI agent definitions, WSL2 runbooks, Cursor native files, and one-day Lovable business-tool delivery.
Requested consistency review, source URLs, phase definitions, agent naming cleanup, and rename from AIFirstSDLC/AIFirestSDLC to AiDosSDLC.
Added standards cookbook/control-manual focus with ISO/IEC/IEEE extraction, gates, checklists, templates, and audit evidence.
Shifted from direct XML/agent definition to cautious foundation-doc-first process because XML grew too complex to control reliably.
Merged and improved foundation docs, project status, completion plan, reference guide, and master Markdown for single-file review.
Focused project content on ADOS_PROJECT and DARKFACTORY placement, Market Go/No-Go gate before BRD, G1 instance layout, and G2 pack schemas/mapping/validator work.

archetype_fields.scope: Governed ADOS/AiDos SDLC foundation documentation, standards alignment, agent roles, Cursor MCP/Skills setup contracts, Dark Factory instance generation, pack schemas, phase gates, traceability, and deterministic validation.

items.title: DisplayDiag and Get-Network-info diagnostic scripts
items.description: Durable PowerShell diagnostic script work for Windows display/GPU, NVIDIA driver, WSL2/Ollama, VPN, Wi-Fi, and network health troubleshooting.

primary_archetype.label: Automation / diagnostic script
primary_archetype.rationale: The accepted deliveries are PowerShell scripts and diagnostic ZIP outputs that collect evidence and automate troubleshooting; they are not user-facing apps. This overrides the candidate software_app.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: Multiple passes interpret collected GPU, driver, WSL, VPN, and network logs into concrete troubleshooting advice.
The WSL2/Ollama download issue included verification of external claims and comparison of likely causes and workarounds.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on Windows PowerShell scripting, command behavior, event log collection, WMI/CIM queries, WSL invocation, packaging, and CLI diagnostics; health_medical is not material.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: Network, WSL2, VPN, and runtime environment diagnostics support reliable local model download and execution workflows.
Ollama and large Qwen model download reliability shaped part of the network diagnostic scope.

items.goal: Create and refine reliable Windows diagnostic scripts and evidence reviews for GPU/display and WSL2 network download troubleshooting.

objectives.text: Collect display, GPU, monitor, EDID, event log, driver, network, VPN, WSL, and Ollama state into reproducible diagnostic bundles.
Reduce long diagnostic runtime by adding timestamps, faster probes, and narrower WSL checks.
Preserve versioned behavior, document atomic requirements, and verify that newer script versions do not drop earlier requirements.

requirements: Provide full code for changed scripts when requested.
Suppress harmless missing-event warnings while preserving diagnostic evidence.
Handle missing or unavailable Windows event channels safely.
Detect when Administrator context misrepresents the normal user WSL/Ollama environment.
Treat Wi-Fi as the preferred path when it is intentionally faster than Ethernet.
Do not flag disabled Ethernet as a fault when disabled by user policy.
Add timestamps or timing evidence to identify slow commands.
Rename network output ZIPs to network-info*.zip.
Restore v1.1.2 no-parameter behavior so the script shows help and exits.
Create atomic requirement review files for compared script versions.

requirements_evolution.change: Started with get-display-info.ps1 for RTX 5070 display/port troubleshooting.
Expanded display diagnostics to event providers, missing-channel handling, and full corrected script output.
Added driver installation troubleshooting and interpretation of DisplayDiag outputs showing Microsoft Basic Display Adapter state.
Shifted to WSL2/Ollama large model download failures, Mozilla VPN, Wi-Fi, and network diagnostic collection.
Reviewed network script versions, decomposed atomic requirements, improved runtime, timestamps, and ZIP naming.
Restored no-parameter help behavior and adjusted policy for Wi-Fi-preferred, kirae-user WSL diagnostics.

archetype_fields.quickstart: Run the relevant PowerShell script with parameters such as -Minutes from the intended Windows user context; use elevated PowerShell only when adapter/event permissions require it.
archetype_fields.what_it_automates: Collects Windows GPU/display, monitor, event log, driver, network adapter, VPN, WSL, Ollama, DNS, routing, and offload evidence into timestamped folders and ZIP archives for troubleshooting.
archetype_fields.how_to_update: Revise the PowerShell script version, preserve documented atomic requirements, retest zero-argument/help behavior, and package updated script, README, and change summary.

items.title: Funny Artist Names
items.description: Iterative generation of funny artist profile names under changing letter, length, geography, and name-pattern constraints.

primary_archetype.label: Content / writing deliverable
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered artifact is lists of candidate profile/account names, not software or a reusable system.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on creative naming constraints, word choice, profile-name suitability, and brand/name pattern fit.

items.goal: Produce funny artist profile names that satisfy specified starting-letter, word-length, and naming-pattern constraints.

objectives.text: Generate candidate two-word artist profile names.
Constrain names by first-letter patterns, total length, and word count.
Add checks such as first letters and lengths after each pair.
Explore alternate naming sources including Russian names, geography-derived surnames, Soviet cartoons, and English first/last names.

requirements: List 20 or 50 funny artist profile/account names or two-word name pairs.
Use two words or name-like pairs unless otherwise requested.
Apply A/F or date-derived F/A starting-letter constraints when specified.
Keep pair length or each word length under the requested limit.
Provide first letters, lengths, or check results when requested.
Use Russian short names, geography-derived surname endings, Soviet cartoon references, or English first/last names in later variants.

requirements_evolution.change: Started with 20 two-word funny artist names split between A-F and F-A patterns, total length under 12 letters.
Added explicit rechecking, first-letter reporting, length reporting, and true/false validation.
Shifted to date-derived placeholders where Friday and April map to F and A.
Explored Russian short first names with geography-derived Russian surname endings.
Explored Soviet cartoon-inspired account names and English first/last name pairs with D/F constraints.

deliveries.name: Constraint checks with letters and lengths

archetype_fields.summary: Generated and revised lists of funny artist profile names under strict letter, length, and source-pattern constraints.

items.title: Funny Artist Names: Criteria
items.description: Generated constrained pairs of English words or short names for funny artist profile names, using date-derived letters and length checks.

primary_archetype.label: Content / writing deliverable
primary_archetype.rationale: The accepted delivery is written naming content: lists of funny artist profile-name pairs with criteria checks.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on creative name generation constrained by initials, word choice, humor, and profile-name suitability.

items.goal: Produce 20 funny artist profile-name pairs that satisfy specified initial-letter distribution and length constraints.

objectives.text: Generate amusing two-word artist profile names using only the allowed initials.
Retest each pair against letter and length criteria before output.
Correct nonmatching pairs immediately when checks fail.

requirements: Use date-derived or supplied letters A and F as the only allowed initials.
Each pair must contain two words separated by a space.
The first and second words must start with different allowed letters.
Exactly half of the pairs must start with one allowed letter and half with the other.
Total length of both words must be less than 12 letters, later refined to between 5 and 12 characters in one pass.
Provide first-letter, word-length, pair-length, and true/false check details when requested.
Correct invalid pairs before or alongside output.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request used Friday and April to derive F and A, with LEN=12 and detailed criteria-test output.
User repeatedly emphasized immediate correction after false criteria tests.
Criteria wording was refined to require different first letters and an even split of pair starting letters.
One later request added a lower length bound, requiring total pair length between 5 and LEN.

archetype_fields.content_type: Constrained list of funny artist profile-name pairs
archetype_fields.audience_and_tone: User seeking playful profile names; terse, checklist-like output with validation details.
archetype_fields.summary: Generated two-word funny artist names using A/F initials, balanced starts, different word initials, length limits, and true/false criteria checks.

items.title: Funny Artist Profile Names
items.description: Generated constrained pairs of English words or short names intended as funny artist profile names, mostly using A/F starting-letter rules and length checks.

primary_archetype.label: Content / writing deliverable
primary_archetype.rationale: The accepted delivery is written name-list content, not an application, schema, or analysis artifact.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness is governed by creative naming constraints for profile names, including word choice, tone, initials, and length.

items.goal: Produce funny artist profile name pairs that satisfy specified initial-letter and length constraints.

objectives.text: Generate pairs of English words or short names suitable for funny artist profile names.
Apply A/F or date-derived initial-letter constraints to control pair structure.
Report letters, lengths, and true/false checks to validate each pair against criteria.

requirements: Use two-word pairs separated as requested.
Words must start with specified letters, primarily A and F or date-derived L1/L2.
The two words in each pair must start with different letters where specified.
Enforce per-word and total-length limits where specified.
Balance first-word starting letters across halves of the list where specified.
Provide criteria fulfillment metadata and corrections when requested.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request asked for 20 two-word funny artist profile names, half A-F and half F-A, with total length under 12 letters.
Added date-derived variables L1 and L2 plus explicit formatting, retesting, and immediate correction requirements.
Refined variables to L1=A and L2=F and required first-half versus second-half starting-letter distribution.
Reduced one request to 10 pairs and added per-word length under 6 letters.
Simplified later requests to 20 pairs with A-F and F-A ordering and per-word length under 6 letters.

deliveries.name: Funny artist profile name pair lists

archetype_fields.content_type: Constrained list of funny artist profile name ideas
archetype_fields.audience_and_tone: For an artist profile; playful, short, and name-like.
archetype_fields.summary: Multiple lists of A/F word pairs were generated under changing constraints for initials, word length, total length, ordering, and validation output.

items.title: Cursor AI Agentic Development PDF Synthesis and Prompt Pack
items.description: Evidence-gated analysis of academic PDFs to extract success factors, failure factors, gates, and prompt clauses for Cursor AI prompt-driven commercial web app development.

objectives.text: Identify high-impact success and failure factors from academic PDFs and rank them by delivery ROI.
Convert recommendations into Cursor prompt clauses, CI gates, checklists, and blueprint documents.
Limit extraction and web research to high-value evidence to avoid exhaustive processing overhead.
Use citations, stable schemas, status tables, no-fabrication rules, and traceability to validate outputs.

archetype_fields.research_question: Which evidence-backed artifacts, gates, prompt clauses, and development practices most improve Cursor AI prompt-driven commercial web app delivery while preventing drift, regressions, ambiguity, and wasted iterations?

items.title: W11 Health Snapshot Diagnostic Script and PC Remediation
items.description: Durable Windows 11 diagnostic effort producing and validating a PowerShell health snapshot collector, baseline helpers, tests, JSON artifacts, and targeted PC remediation guidance.

primary_archetype.label: Automation / diagnostic script
primary_archetype.rationale: The durable delivery is a PowerShell diagnostic toolchain that collects Windows/system health evidence and supports troubleshooting; there is no user-facing app workflow.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: The script extracts Windows event, device, storage, security, network, GPU, and process data into structured JSON outputs.
The conversations also diagnose and prioritize fixes for Defender, SMB security, network adapters, storage/RAID noise, GPU PCIe data, and BIOS update steps.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on reliable collectors, parseable JSON outputs, manifests, error handling, baselines, and tests for a diagnostic data pipeline.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: Defender status, SMB guest/anonymous exposure, WFP captures, and security summaries materially affect findings and risk.
Repeated reviews focused on Pester tests, elevated/non-elevated runs, ZIP failure handling, and manifest correctness.

items.goal: Maintain a production-usable Windows 11 health snapshot script that captures enough structured system evidence to troubleshoot PC health issues across elevated and non-elevated runs.

objectives.text: Collect comprehensive Windows 11 health evidence into parseable, versioned JSON snapshots and ZIP artifacts.
Reduce false positives through machine-local PnP baselines and confidence-aware health hints.
Validate each script revision with elevated and non-elevated runs plus automated tests.
Use collected evidence to prioritize concrete PC fixes without generic resets.

requirements: Collect OS, security, storage, network, display/GPU, PnP, boot/shutdown, event log, process, task, and health hint data.
Emit robust JSON with normalized empty outputs and no corrupt files.
Keep collector_errors.json clean except for real unexpected failures.
Support elevated and regular-user execution with expected permission handling.
Avoid duplicate NVIDIA raw output and distinguish active RTX 3090 PCIe data.
Preserve raw PnP evidence while allowing local baselines to lower health-hint severity.
Track saved files and ZIP/archive failures accurately in manifests and collector errors.
Provide Pester coverage for baseline behavior, snapshot defaults, ZIP behavior, and pattern upserts.
Do not rely on GitHub commits being made by Cursor when user requested local fixes only.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial focus was PC health triage and identifying both environment fixes and diagnostic script gaps.
Scope expanded to validating fixed W11 Health Snapshot outputs, GitHub repo code, elevated/non-elevated behavior, GPU/storage/security collectors, and test coverage.
Added machine-local PnP baseline requirements to suppress known intentional device issues without hiding raw evidence.
Added fixes for ZIP error persistence, saved_files_manifest accuracy, baseline metadata semantics, and current-run ZIP tests.
Environment remediation extended to Dell Precision 5820 BIOS update verification and post-update Windows health checks.

archetype_fields.quickstart: Run Get-W11HealthSnapshot.ps1, preferably once elevated and once as a regular user, then review the generated W11Health_*.zip JSON artifacts and collector_errors.json.
archetype_fields.what_it_automates: Collects Windows 11/system health evidence including events, security, storage, PnP, network, GPU/PCIe, boot/shutdown, processes, tasks, manifests, and health hints for later troubleshooting.
archetype_fields.how_to_update: Modify the PowerShell collectors and baseline helpers in the repo, run the Pester tests, then verify with elevated and non-elevated snapshot ZIPs before committing.

items.title: Windows 11 Event Log and TPM/Hello/BitLocker Troubleshooting
items.description: Multi-pass diagnosis of Windows 11 event logs, handle counts, Task Scheduler COM-handler failures, storage events, TPM, Windows Hello, and BitLocker evidence with prioritized remediation guidance.

primary_archetype.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered value is diagnostic interpretation and concrete fix guidance for Windows PC configuration issues, not the scripts or JSON files themselves.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: PowerShell collection scripts and exported diagnostics supported repeated evidence capture.
Event logs, process snapshots, handle dumps, registry exports, and TPM/BitLocker data were transformed into structured evidence for analysis.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on Windows system diagnostics, event-log interpretation, PowerShell commands, Task Scheduler behavior, COM registration, storage stack signals, and service/process evidence.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: TPM, Windows Hello for Business, BitLocker, DPAPI, and NCrypt findings affect authentication and device encryption posture.

items.goal: Identify the most severe Windows 11 configuration or reliability issues after reboot and provide evidence-based steps to fix or rule them out.

objectives.text: Classify severe event-log clusters and separate actionable issues from benign startup noise.
Provide targeted PowerShell commands to collect missing evidence and avoid broad guessing.
Validate hypotheses using event IDs, handle counts, task XML, COM CLSID mappings, registry exports, and TPM/BitLocker evidence.

requirements: Analyze Windows event summaries and attached JSON/XML/TXT diagnostic outputs.
Prioritize severe issues and suggest concrete remediation steps.
Explain the meaning of Task Scheduler 0x80070004 and 0x80040154 failures.
Determine whether total handle counts indicate a system-wide handle leak.
Map scheduled task COM handler CLSIDs to registered DLLs.
Investigate TPM, Windows Hello for Business, BitLocker, Crypto-NCrypt, and DPAPI events.
Use supplied deterministic artifacts without inventing file names, versions, or dates.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request focused on 3676 processed Windows events and severe actions.
Investigation narrowed to StorPort 549 storage errors and Task Scheduler failures with 0x80070004.
Handle-count diagnostics were added to test whether failures came from a handle leak.
Task XML and registry CLSID exports were added to map COM-handler scheduled tasks.
Post-reboot analysis expanded to Windows 11 configuration, TPM, Hello for Business, BitLocker, Crypto-NCrypt, and DPAPI evidence.

archetype_fields.symptom: Windows 11 showed severe-looking event clusters after reboot, including StorPort 549 errors, Task Scheduler COM-handler failures, missing expected perf counter path, and TPM/Hello/BitLocker/Crypto events.
archetype_fields.resolution: Guidance ruled out a global handle leak from normal total handle counts, mapped scheduled task CLSIDs to system DLLs, recommended targeted event XML, registry, process, handle, storage, TPM, Hello, and BitLocker evidence collection, and prioritized storage/COM/security findings over benign startup noise.
archetype_fields.still_open: Final root cause is not fully proven from the reduced transcript; storage reliability, COM activation failure causes, and TPM/Hello/BitLocker state require continued validation with the collected artifacts.

items.goal: Create a best-in-class lightweight AP exam drill web tool with extensible JSON question packs, high-quality AP-style questions, mobile-friendly quiz modes, and exportable session evidence for later training analysis.

objectives.text: Build an iPhone-friendly dark quiz app with free-text and multiple-choice answer modes.

Track session progress in browser memory and export attempts to TXT for AI review.
Improve AP Biology and AP Micro question quality, distractors, explanations, abbreviations, and pack naming.
Review AP Biology items against official AP source logic to detect semantic drift and preserve scoring meaning.
Use Lovable prompts and corrective prompts to speed app iteration without manual full implementation.

requirements: Questions should support free-text answers and MCQ mode with exactly three close wrong answers.
Correct MCQ answer should be randomly placed in the GUI.
User progress should be tracked in browser session memory without a database.
Questions should be stored in JSON files and be easy to extend by adding new packs.
The app should support selectable domains including AP Biology, AP Physics 1, and AP Micro, with AP Physics not newly populated in the later update.
Session exports should include questions, user answers, correct answers, and results in TXT for later AI analysis.
Question answers should remain short where required, generally one answer of no more than three words.
Pack names should be short, coherent, and omit AP exam names.
Abbreviations appearing in questions should be explained in explanations.
Question quality must not be downgraded during cleanup.
AP Biology semantic alignment should preserve subject identity, baseline, causal chain, directionality, scope, and scoring logic from official AP source concepts without copying wording.

Expanded to very hard AP Biology questions for Units 1 and 2, then all units, limited to seven complex questions per unit.

Reviewed GitHub implementation and iterated on domain selector, AP Micro packs, GUI, logic, and question quality.

Requested UX diagnosis for dropdown flicker/twitch and a corrective Lovable prompt.
Requested abbreviation expansion in explanations, normalized pack names, quality-preserving JSON cleanup, ZIP output, and extra review.
Requested semantic alignment of AP Biology app questions with closest official AP Biology source questions or scoring guidelines, with corrected JSONs, ZIP, and report.
Identified ambiguity in a migration/resource availability question and reframed QA around official-source semantic diff and mandatory source mapping.

Provide practical troubleshooting steps such as iDrive reboot, GPS/privacy checks, open-area testing, updates, and service escalation.
Confirm uncertain model-specific procedures online, including GPS status availability and iDrive reboot duration.

requirements: Diagnose navigation features disappearing on a 2023 BMW i4.
Explain why the map may appear but remain stuck at the home location.
Clarify how to verify GPS/location settings in the user's visible iDrive screens.
Confirm whether a direct GPS Status or Location Information option exists for the model.
Verify the correct iDrive reboot procedure and duration.
Address slow or failed navigation startup despite updated software, especially possible system overload.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial troubleshooting expanded from missing navigation features to GPS/location inaccuracy and iDrive reboot verification.
Issue evolved to navigation not starting or starting slowly, with focus on reducing possible iDrive system overload.

deliveries.name: Model-specific iDrive GPS and reboot clarification

archetype_fields.symptom: BMW i4 2023 navigation disappeared, later showed the map but failed to determine current location, and later started slowly or not at all.
archetype_fields.resolution: Restart iDrive with the volume/mute button for about 30 seconds, verify privacy/GPS tracking settings, test GPS in an open area, check updates and map/navigation data, reduce connected devices/background load, and contact BMW service if GPS remains unavailable.
archetype_fields.still_open: Exact on-screen GPS diagnostic availability remained limited; persistent GPS failure may require BMW service diagnostics.

items.title: AdosWriter and AdosDocEvaluator Directives
items.description: A durable set of AI-agent directives and standalone SRS/document-evaluation templates for staged domain writing, SRS generation, and compact document scoring within an ADOS workflow.

primary_archetype.label: AI agent / prompt / skill
primary_archetype.rationale: The main delivered artifacts are system directives defining AI-agent roles, stages, outputs, evaluation rules, and behavior contracts, not an end-user software application.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: The work defines governed artifacts, evaluation contracts, stage manifests, question registers, and SRS template structures used as controlled execution documents.
Standalone Markdown SRS templates and improvement notes are written deliverables intended for reuse in prompt attachments.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on prompt/directive design, staged agent behavior, structured outputs, evaluation gates, and prevention of instruction drift.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: The SRS and evaluator content concerns requirements quality, traceability, pass/fail gates, scoring, and implementation-readiness for software/tool specifications.

items.goal: Create production-ready ADOS directives and templates that make AI agents write, clarify, evaluate, and compare domain documents through explicit staged workflows and traceable scoring.

objectives.text: Define AdosWriter as a staged domain-writing directive with PREPARE, CLARIFY, and EXCELLENCE stages.
Add reusable document-evaluation logic for scoring one or several similar documents and selecting winners compactly.
Use closed/open question artifacts, manifests, evaluation contracts, and pass/fail gates to preserve traceability and control drift.
Keep outputs practical by providing downloadable full directive/template files and limiting comparison displays to top documents.

requirements: AdosWriter must support a three-stage workflow with explicit stage artifacts.
User clarification must distinguish open, closed, resolved, assumed, and deferred question states.
Evaluation must be field-driven and should not duplicate success criteria in ways that weaken the directive model.
Candidate evaluation must ensure failing candidates cannot be selected as final winners.
AdosDocEvaluator must support single-document scoring and comparison of multiple similar documents.
Evaluation contracts must have definitive fill rules and fields that materially affect scoring.
Comparison output must use short document slugs and show only the top three documents when more than three are loaded.
Alternative scoring may be included only if it improves winner selection without overloading visible output.
The SRS template must be standalone, self-contained, suitable as an AI-agent prompt attachment, and aligned for AI-agent usage.
AdosWriter workflow must govern the writing process without automatically transforming the target artifact into an AdosWriter-specific artifact unless requested.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial AdosWriter directive was hardened with explicit contracts, loop controls, structured outputs, evaluation rubric, and failure conditions.
Directive evolved from a two-stage to a three-stage model with user loop, field-driven evaluation, pass/fail gates, and staged artifacts.
Closed question handling was revised around closed_questions_stage2.json and traceability from Stage 1 to Stage 2.
A standalone SRS template was improved for AI-agent usage and later diagnosed for drift when AdosWriter workflow was confused with AdosWriter-specific template transformation.
AdosDocEvaluator was split into a separate directive while AdosWriter Step 3.2 was minimally patched for reusable document evaluation.
AdosDocEvaluator was enhanced with revised evaluation_contract rules, scoring weights, short slugs, top-three display policy, and lightweight alternative scoring.

archetype_fields.role_or_persona: AdosWriter domain-writing agent and AdosDocEvaluator document-scoring agent.
archetype_fields.instructions_summary: Use staged preparation, clarification, candidate generation, field-driven evaluation, pass/fail gates, question lifecycle tracking, compact scoring tables, and traceable artifacts to produce or evaluate documents.
archetype_fields.how_to_use: Attach the directive and relevant template or documents to an AI agent prompt, specify the target document or comparison task, and require the agent to follow the declared stages and output contracts.

Write events.json as one event object per line.

events.json format changed to full-event JSON Lines with selected fields preserved and truncation metadata removed.
Script hardening added to fix failures around missing Count, LogName, and RecordId properties.

archetype_fields.quickstart: Run get-events-and-performance-data.ps1 in elevated PowerShell with options such as -Minutes, -MaxEvent, -ExtendedEvents, -WithServices, -WithPnp, -WithTasks, -IncludeSecurity, -WithUsers, -WithApps, and -WithVolumes.

items.title: LangChain, OpenRouter, Cursor, and Hybrid Legal LLM Architectures
items.description: Factual Q&A explaining LangChain and OpenRouter, Cursor integration options, business uses of LangChain, and hybrid architectures using Hugging Face legal models.

primary_archetype.label: Knowledge Q&A / explanation
primary_archetype.rationale: The item is a sequence of explanatory answers to conceptual and practical questions, with no durable artifact or runnable deliverable.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on understanding LLM application frameworks, API gateways, IDE model integration, RAG architecture, vector stores, and deployment tradeoffs.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: The discussion covers Hugging Face model use, local versus cloud inference, embeddings, rerankers, and hybrid legal-model deployment.
One portion evaluates profitable business automation use cases such as customer support, document AI, and internal workflow automation.

items.goal: Understand how LangChain, OpenRouter, Cursor, and Hugging Face legal models can be used together for LLM applications and business automation.

objectives.text: Explain what LangChain and OpenRouter are and how they compare with alternatives.
Clarify whether and how Cursor can use OpenRouter-compatible model endpoints.
Identify high-value business tasks LangChain can automate.
Describe hybrid legal-document architectures that balance local storage/search with large hosted Hugging Face models.
Compare cost and deployment options for cloud, local, and hybrid model usage.

requirements: Answer in Russian to user questions about LangChain.ai, OpenRouter.ai, Cursor AI, Hugging Face legal models, and hybrid deployment.
Explain alternatives, integration paths, benefits, limitations, and costs at a practical level.
Include examples of popular and profitable LangChain business automation scenarios.
Explain how private databases and documents connect through loaders, vector stores, retrievers, and model calls.

requirements_evolution.change: Started with a general comparison of LangChain, OpenRouter, alternatives, and Cursor compatibility.
Expanded into verified-style discussion of Cursor model provider support and OpenRouter setup via OpenAI-compatible base URL.
Shifted to business use cases LangChain can automate.
Added private database/document connection architecture using loaders, chunking, vector stores, and retrievers.
Extended to Hugging Face legal models and cost models for cloud, local, and hybrid operation.

deliveries.name: Hybrid Hugging Face legal model architecture
Cost comparison for model deployment options

archetype_fields.question: What are LangChain.ai and OpenRouter.ai, how can they work with Cursor and Hugging Face legal models, what business tasks can LangChain automate, and what are the costs and architecture of hybrid legal-document solutions?
archetype_fields.answer_summary: LangChain is an LLM application framework for chains, agents, data connectors, RAG, and tool orchestration. OpenRouter is an OpenAI-compatible gateway for accessing many model providers through one API key and billing layer. Cursor can sometimes use OpenRouter by overriding the OpenAI base URL, with model and feature limitations. LangChain is useful for customer support automation, enterprise search/document AI, and workflow automation. Private documents connect through loaders, chunking, embeddings, vector stores, and retrievers; databases connect through SQL/NoSQL connectors. Hugging Face legal models can be used via hosted inference or locally, with hybrid designs keeping storage, indexing, and embeddings local while calling large hosted legal LLMs only for complex final reasoning to control cost.

items.title: Gemini AQA API call from ps1 script
items.description: Troubleshot Google Generative Language AQA generateAnswer curl requests in PowerShell, ending with a working inline_passages payload that answered a France question and clarified semantic_retriever corpus requirements.

primary_archetype.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered value was diagnostic guidance and corrected request JSON for a concrete API error sequence, not a reusable app or package.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depended on Gemini/AQA question-answering request structure, grounding configuration, and model input limits.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: PowerShell curl syntax and JSON payload construction were necessary to execute the API calls.

items.goal: Create a working PowerShell curl example for Google AQA generateAnswer using valid grounding input and understand the limits of inline passages versus semantic retrieval.

objectives.text: Diagnose invalid JSON field errors for AQA generateAnswer requests.
Identify the correct inline_passages schema using Content.parts.text.
Clarify that semantic_retriever requires an existing corpus and cannot directly use a live Wikipedia URL.
Iterate from API errors to a confirmed successful response returning Paris.

requirements: Use the Generative Language endpoint https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/aqa:generateAnswer?key=$KEY.
Do not use a top-level grounding_source field.
For semantic_retriever, source must be an existing corpus or document resource such as corpora/{id}.
A raw HTTPS URL cannot be used directly as semantic_retriever.source or as inline passage content fetch target.
For inline_passages, each passage must include an id and content as a structured Content object with parts containing text.
Use models/aqa as the model reference in the request body or endpoint context.
Inline passage size is governed by practical model input/context limits rather than a clearly documented per-field maximum.

requirements_evolution.change: Started with invalid grounding_source usage and moved to semantic_retriever investigation.
Determined semantic_retriever requires pre-created corpora and cannot directly consume https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/France.
Switched to inline_passages and corrected passage content from string/text/passage fields to Content.parts.text.
Confirmed successful AQA response returning Paris with grounding attribution.

deliveries.name: Working PowerShell curl payload for AQA inline_passages

archetype_fields.symptom: AQA generateAnswer curl requests returned INVALID_ARGUMENT errors for grounding_source, inline_passages fields, semantic_retriever source, and passage content shape.
archetype_fields.resolution: Use models/aqa with inline_passages.passages containing id and content as a Content object with parts text; use semantic_retriever only with an existing corpus resource.
archetype_fields.still_open: Exact documented maximum size for inline_passages.passages.content.parts.text was not identified.

items.goal: Make the Diet-Microbiome-Cognition project defensible for a near-term science fair presentation by aligning the app, claims, data, and judge materials with credible computational biology practice.

objectives.text: Convert the first app version into a concise offline scientific research interface suitable for judges.
Use the existing student pilot dataset and public/literature evidence without overclaiming unavailable biomarker data.

Add validation framing around leakage prevention, CLR transforms, limitations, and reproducible summaries.
Prepare judge-facing wording such as errata/update and abstract revisions to reduce claim mismatch risk.

requirements: Support offline/local use on a Macbook in case internet is unavailable during judging.
Represent the project as computational biology/bioinformatics, not as completed clinical proof.
Use the n=66 student dataset only for diet-score and cognitive-score pilot analysis.
Do not claim measured microbiome or metabolite biomarkers in the student pilot dataset.
Focus biological interpretation on SCFAs and neurotransmitter precursors.
Acknowledge limitations, including missing biomarker data, small sample size, and literature-limited Stage 3.
Avoid data leakage and describe validation honestly.
Provide a simple, modern, laconic interface appropriate for AI-assisted scientific research.
Include app features that better match the PDF, overview, and abstract while remaining rule-compliant.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request sought literature-backed critique of a 3-stage Diet -> Microbiome -> Metabolites -> Cognition ML pipeline and app improvement prompt.
Scope tightened after recognizing the app was a credible research demo rather than fully validated research.

User requested judge-facing errata/update and substantial app changes rather than only advice.
User requested abstract review and emphasized that the app and AI-assisted interface should be the main competitive strength.

items.title: WSL2 journal cleanup
items.description: Troubleshooting and cleanup of WSL2 systemd journal growth, noisy services, boot warnings, and current-boot error investigation.

primary_archetype.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered value is diagnostic guidance and remediation steps for WSL2 journald/systemd behavior, not a user-facing application.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: Diagnostic shell scripts were produced or retained to check WSL boot cleanliness, journal health, and log triage.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on Linux/WSL system administration, systemd, journald retention, service state, boot diagnostics, and host networking configuration.

items.goal: Bring the WSL2 Ubuntu instance to a bounded journal state and a practically clean boot with fixable current-boot errors resolved or isolated.

objectives.text: Identify where WSL2 journal logs are stored and how to query today/current-boot messages.
Reduce and bound journald disk usage through vacuuming and effective configuration checks.
Find noisy or failed services such as wsl-pro.service, cron, AppArmor sysctl config, snapd, Ollama, WSLg, and networking checks.
Provide concise commands for validation, reboot checks, and future log triage.
Confirm applied settings through systemd-analyze, journalctl disk usage, service states, and current-boot logs.

requirements: Explain WSL2 journald storage and journalctl usage for today/current boot.
Safely reduce journal disk usage and explain why vacuum/config changes may not immediately change active files.
Create or verify journald limits and later volatile storage behavior.
Disable or fix unneeded noisy services, especially wsl-pro.service.
Review shutdown/start results and distinguish real errors from WSL virtualization noise.
Investigate all realistic current-boot errors without breaking normal CLI/dev workflow.
Explain where Windows-side .wslconfig lives and how to create/edit it for VPN-related WSL networking behavior.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request focused on locating WSL2 journal files, exporting today's messages, and cleaning a large journal.
Expanded to permanent journald retention limits and commands to investigate warnings/errors.
Added verification of effective journald config and interpretation of attached journal zip outputs.
Shifted to clean WSL2 reboot validation after disabling wsl-pro.service and tuning journal behavior.
Expanded to fixing all realistic current-boot errors and Windows-side .wslconfig guidance due to Mozilla VPN networking concerns.

archetype_fields.symptom: WSL2 Ubuntu journal grew large and current-boot logs showed noisy warnings/errors from journald retention, wsl-pro.service, boot/user-session messages, optional services, and possible VPN-related networking checks.
archetype_fields.resolution: Reduced journal size, added effective journald limits/volatile storage, disabled unneeded wsl-pro.service and ollama.service, checked clean boot state with systemd/journalctl commands, and provided targeted remediation and .wslconfig guidance.
archetype_fields.still_open: Final confirmation of all current-boot errors after WSL update and .wslconfig/VPN networking changes remains open.

items.title: Remove large file from Git history
items.description: Troubleshooting guidance for deleting an accidentally committed large file from local and remote Git history using git-filter-repo, force push, and fresh clone workflow.

primary_archetype.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
primary_archetype.rationale: The interaction resolves a concrete Git problem through diagnostic explanation and step-by-step advice, not by delivering a runnable app or durable artifact.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on Git command-line behavior, repository history rewriting, remotes, clones, and collaborator impacts.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: Removing a large or sensitive committed file from repository history can reduce unintended exposure, though the main issue is Git operations.

items.goal: Completely remove an accidentally committed large file from local and remote Git repository history while understanding the consequences for other clones.

objectives.text: Explain how to remove the file from Git history with git-filter-repo and why --invert-paths is required.
Provide alternatives and installation guidance when git-filter-repo is unavailable or refuses to run on a non-fresh clone.
Clarify force-push effects on the remote repository and required collaborator cleanup after history rewrite.

requirements: Remove random_forest_weather_model.pkl or another specified large file from repository history.
Use --invert-paths so only the target file is removed and the rest of the repository is preserved.

Suppress low-amplitude frequencies embedded inside high-amplitude audio, not merely silent time regions.
Reduce high-frequency hiss and nearly inaudible spectral components.
Use a free VST3-compatible spectral gate on Windows 64-bit.
Avoid unnecessary loss of useful track loudness.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request focused on using Audacity Compressor to suppress low-amplitude sounds.
Clarified that the target was low-amplitude frequencies overlaid on louder frequencies, requiring spectral or multiband processing.
Shifted from ReaFIR installation to a supported or practical Audacity plugin approach.
Selected Diet Audio Free VST3 as the practical spectral gate candidate for Audacity 3.7.3.
Refined Diet Audio settings after observing that Transients below 0 dB reduced overall track loudness.

deliveries.name: Diet Audio Free VST3 usage guidance

archetype_fields.symptom: The user wanted to remove low-amplitude spectral components and hiss in Audacity, including weak frequencies masked by louder audio; compressor, noise gate, and plugin installation behavior caused confusion.
archetype_fields.resolution: Explained the difference between compression, gating, and spectral gating; recommended Diet Audio Free VST3 for Audacity 3.7.3; advised using Transients at 0 dB when full loudness is desired, tuning Threshold around the useful noise cutoff, and using Normalize or the available Limiter make-up target afterward if needed.
archetype_fields.still_open: Exact final Threshold and other Diet Audio settings remain track-dependent and must be verified by listening and waveform or spectrogram inspection.

items.title: Event review and security analysis
items.description: One-off review of Windows event logs to identify suspicious activity, explain observed events, and recommend security logging improvements.

primary_archetype.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered value was diagnostic interpretation of Windows security events and concrete advice on what was benign, suspicious, or worth changing.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: Export-EventDetails-ByProvider.ps1 supported collection of Windows event data for review.
Windows event records were exported and reviewed in structured log/JSON/CSV form.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on interpreting Windows authentication, firewall, WMI, PowerShell, and audit-policy events for security significance.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: PowerShell event export and structured log processing shaped the evidence available for analysis.

items.goal: Determine whether recent Windows events indicate suspicious activity and identify practical security logging or configuration improvements.

objectives.text: Classify failed logons, firewall rule deletions, WMI events, PowerShell logging, and audit-policy changes as benign or concerning.
Use targeted PowerShell queries and untruncated JSON logs to refine conclusions quickly.
Recommend audit-policy and logging changes to preserve visibility and improve future investigation quality.

requirements: Review Windows event exports from the 6-hour window.
Identify suspicious or security-relevant events to investigate.
Explain whether UAC-related 4625 failures and Chrome Sandbox firewall rule deletions are benign.
Use untruncated JSON events when available for stronger conclusions.
Advise improvements to Windows security auditing and logging.

requirements_evolution.change: Initial request asked for event review and security issue identification.
Additional PowerShell query outputs were provided to clarify failed logons, firewall deletions, VM context, and WMI errors.
Untruncated JSON Windows events were provided to support a firmer conclusion, including audit-policy changes.

archetype_fields.symptom: Windows security and system events included failed logons, firewall rule deletions, WMI errors, PowerShell script-block events, and audit-policy changes that needed security interpretation.
archetype_fields.resolution: Most failed logons and Chrome Sandbox firewall deletions were assessed as likely benign, while security audit-policy removal was flagged as concerning unless intentional; recommendations focused on restoring and improving audit visibility.
archetype_fields.still_open: Confirm whether the Administrator audit-policy changes were intentional and whether Chrome updates or policy cleanup explain the firewall rule deletions.

items.title: Find word with context
items.description: Guidance for searching a huge one-line JSON file on Windows 11 without dumping the full line, including grep/ripgrep options, fixed-string context extraction, locale fixes, secure installation, and WinGet troubleshooting.

primary_archetype.label: Advisory / troubleshooting
primary_archetype.rationale: The delivered value is diagnostic guidance and concrete fixes for Windows command-line search, package installation, and security concerns rather than an accepted application.

secondary_archetypes.distinct_contribution: Provided reusable command wrappers and snippets for extracting fixed-byte context around fixed-string matches.

primary_domain_pair.rationale: Correctness depends on Windows shell behavior, grep/ripgrep flags, regex/fixed-string search semantics, byte offsets, and package-manager usage.

secondary_domain_pairs.distinct_contribution: Included supply-chain and malware-verification guidance for installing ripgrep via WinGet or official releases.

items.goal: Enable reliable, fast, and safe text search with bounded context in a very large one-line JSON file on Windows 11.

objectives.text: Identify tools and commands that can search a huge single-line JSON file without printing the whole line.
Prefer faster fixed-string search and byte slicing when regex context is too slow.
Verify tool installation sources, hashes, and WinGet trust limits to reduce malware risk.

requirements: Search for literal text in a roughly 500 MB one-line JSON file on Windows 11.
Avoid dumping the entire matching JSON line.
Support bounded context before and after matches.
Prefer simple fixed-string search when regex is unnecessary.
Handle Git Bash grep wrapper and locale issues.
Install ripgrep safely and understand WinGet trust boundaries.

requirements_evolution.change: Started with finding a word plus character context in a huge one-line JSON file using Windows tools.
Shifted from regex grep to fixed-string fgrep because PCRE was slow on the large file.
Added tool-selection and secure ripgrep installation guidance, including WinGet failure troubleshooting.
Confirmed that plain fgrep prints the entire matching line and supplied alternatives that suppress or bound output.

archetype_fields.symptom: Need to find literal text with nearby context in a huge one-line JSON file on Windows 11; grep/ripgrep setup caused wrapper, locale, speed, security, and WinGet errors.
archetype_fields.resolution: Use fixed-string search with suppressed or bounded output: grep -aFq for existence, grep -aFc for counts, grep -aFo for matches, or grep -aobF plus byte slicing for context; use ripgrep as the preferred Windows tool when installed safely.
archetype_fields.still_open: True Unicode character-count context remains harder with byte slicing; WinGet 0x80070032 may require App Installer/source reset or an alternative trusted install path.

items.title: Аудио для заучивания текста

Provide slower and segmented versions for easier memorization practice.

Generate audio from the uploaded script.
Support memorization by Yana.
Provide a segmented pack of shorter MP3s with matching text files.
